5.anchor: but gives grace to the humble.”

9.anchor: Stand firm against him, and be strong in your faith. Remember that your family of believers

12.anchor: I have written and sent this short letter to you with the help of Silas,

13.anchor: Your sister church here in Babylon
13.note: Greek The elect one in Babylon.

8.anchor: Finally, all of you should be of one mind. Sympathize with each other. Love each other as brothers and sisters.

16.anchor: But do this in a gentle and respectful way.
16.note: Some English translations put this sentence in verse 15.

18.anchor: for our sins once for all time. He never sinned, but he died for sinners to bring you safely home to God. He suffered physical death, but he was raised to life in the Spirit.

20.anchor: those who disobeyed God long ago when God waited patiently while Noah was building his boat. Only eight people were saved from drowning in that terrible flood.

21.anchor: And that water is a picture of baptism, which now saves you, not by removing dirt from your body, but as a response to God from
21.note: Or as an appeal to God for.

5.anchor: And you are living stones that God is building into his spiritual temple. What’s more, you are his holy priests.

6.anchor: “I am placing a cornerstone in Jerusalem,

7.anchor: Yes, you who trust him recognize the honor God has given him.
7.note: Or Yes, for you who believe, there is honor.

8.anchor: the rock that makes them fall.”

9.anchor: But you are not like that, for you are a chosen people. You are royal priests,

10.anchor: now you have received God’s mercy.”

12.anchor: Be careful to live properly among your unbelieving neighbors. Then even if they accuse you of doing wrong, they will see your honorable behavior, and they will give honor to God when he judges the world.
12.note: Or on the day of visitation.

17.anchor: Respect everyone, and love the family of believers.

18.anchor: You who are slaves must submit to your masters with all respect.
18.note: Or because you fear God; in all fear.

21.anchor: For God called you to do good, even if it means suffering, just as Christ suffered

1.anchor: So then, since Christ suffered physical pain, you must arm yourselves with the same attitude he had, and be ready to suffer, too. For if you have suffered physically for Christ, you have finished with sin.
1.note: Or For the one One who has suffered physically has finished with sin.

6.anchor: That is why the Good News was preached to those who are now dead
6.note: Greek preached even to the dead.

6.anchor: —so although they were destined to die like all people,
6.note: Or so although people had judged them worthy of death.

6.anchor: they now live forever with God in the Spirit.

14.anchor: If you are insulted because you bear the name of Christ, you will be blessed, for the glorious Spirit of God
14.note: Or for the glory of God, which is his Spirit.
Some manuscripts add On their part he is blasphemed, but on your part he is glorified.

18.anchor: what will happen to godless sinners?”

1.anchor: I am writing to God’s chosen people who are living as foreigners in the provinces of Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia.
1.note: and Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, Bithynia

6.note: Or So you are truly glad.

16.anchor: For the Scriptures say, “You must be holy because I am holy.”

22.anchor: You were cleansed from your sins when you obeyed the truth, so now you must show sincere love to each other as brothers and sisters.
Love each other deeply with all your heart.
22.note: Some manuscripts read with a pure heart.

24.anchor: But the word of the Lord remains forever.”
